It is native to tropical Africa, specifically in the countries of Madagascar, the Seychelles, and parts of the African mainland. intro: Stenogrammitis boivinii, also known as Boivin's stenogramma, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical forests of Madagascar and the Mascarene Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Stenogrammitis boivinii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of essential oils and extracts.
